rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHEOL HOON & BUJA LEE LIVING TRUST UNDER AGREEMENT TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To File Portions Of Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to File Portions of Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement Under Seal is denied. Respondent has not carried his burden of showing an overriding interest or a substantial probability of prejudice to that interest as required by Rule of Court 2.550(d). There is, of course, a presumption that court proceedings, including filings, are open to the public, and this is a significant right of constitutional dimension. To overcome that presumption, there must be particularized evidence of "serious injury," shown with specificity, and "[b]road allegations of harm, bereft of specific examples or articulated reasoning, are insuffficient." See Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1282 (quoting In re Cendent Corp. (3rd Cir. 2001) 260 F.3d 183, 194). Without this specific showing of serious injury, neither the mere agreement of the parties nor an appeal to a general privacy right is insufficient to support a sealing order. Id. Here, Respondent has made only the most generalized argument based on a general right of privacy in financial information--to wit the amount he would pay in settlement. His argument is no different than the argument that might be made by any settling party required to make a payment, and he has offered no evidence whatsoever as to how he would be prejudiced by revealing the redacted information. The tax characterization issue and the amount he is alleged to have received are already in the public record. The motion to seal is denied. The unredacted records lodged under seal are ordered returned to Respondent. The parties have seven days to file any additional papers in support of the pending petition to approve the settlement agreement. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, February 5, 2019.
